engine.ladder_reasoning: Single hero: one headline, one paragraph, one button to an external Tally form. There is essentially no persuasion apparatus (no credentials, process, or proof) — the page only ASKS for the one action, which is the quick-yes signature.

goal.one_action: apply for a 1-1 Growth Sprint by filling the external Tally form

structure.archetype_note: Unrouted single page, no title/meta/nav/footer; one hero block + one outbound CTA. Link-in-bio-grade minimalism.

mapped.observed_as: Build AI Systems that Power Growth

confidence.why: fetch_quality is full but the page is extremely thin (one hero, no proof/nav) — quick-yes vs trust hinges on the near-total absence of persuasion; capture is complete so classification is clear but sparse.

gap_findings: CTA hands off to an off-site Tally application form (redirect to external form tool); expressible as delegated 'form' mechanism, no new capability needed
